Document:
# PALADIN-CLASS ASSAULT SHUTTLE
[color=#a4885c]Ship Size:[/color] Small
[color=#a4885c]Fleet Shuttle:[/color] No
[color=#a4885c]Recommended Crew:[/color] 1-2
[color=#a4885c]Power Gen Type:[/color] APU
[color=#a4885c]Expeditions:[/color] No
[color=#a4885c]IFF Console:[/color] Yes
[color=#a4885c]Restrictions:[/color] Deputy
"A small security assault craft designed for space combat equipped with an EXP-2100g mounted grenade launcher."
# Ship Functions
- There are two buttons at the front of the ship: The button with the yellow and black frame controls the shutters located at the front, while the button with the red and black frame controls the mounted grenade launcher.
- There is a remote signaller linked to the grenade launcher in addition to the button, allowing you to fire it quickly in the middle of a pursuit.
# Tips on running NSF Paladin shuttles - The Paladin supports up to [color=#a4885c]12 [bold]kW[/bold][/color] on its shuttle APUs. It is not recommended to go above this due to the small interior space. For more general information on the power grid and other life support systems, refer to the [textlink="Power" link="Power"] guidebook article and the [textlink="Preflight Checklist" link="PreflightChecklist"/]. - For the basics of [bold]shuttle piloting[/bold], refer to [textlink="Piloting" link="Piloting"] and [textlink="Sector Topology" link="SectorTopology"/]. - For the basics of [bold]cargo hauling[/bold], refer to [textlink="Cargo Hauling" link="CargoHauling"]. - For the basics of [bold]expedition diving[/bold], refer to [textlink="Expeditions" link="Expeditions"]. - As a Captain of a Nanotrasen vessel, you have the authority to hire, fire, demote, or promote crew members at will, refer to [textlink="Hiring" link="Hiring"] for more information.
